channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Arsenal eye Kroupi & Rogers - and a new Arteta deal
item.description: After their long-awaited Premier League title triumph, senior football correspondent Sami Mokbel assesses Arsenal's to-do list for this summer.

item.title: Southampton owner will not sack head coach Eckert
item.description: Southampton owner Dragan Solak says he will not sack Tonda Eckert, despite the "mistake" the head coach made when authorising a spying campaign against rival Championship clubs.

item.title: Keegan reveals stage four cancer diagnosis
item.description: Former England captain and manager Kevin Keegan reveals he has stage four cancer.

item.title: 'You legend. Manager loved it' - Southampton's Spygate WhatsApps revealed
item.description: New WhatsApp messages have revealed how Southampton orchestrated a spying campaign against their Championship rivals.

item.title: Chelsea reject approaches for Acheampong
item.description: Chelsea reject multiple approaches for promising young defender Josh Acheampong who the club consider one of their 'untouchables'.

item.title: Fenerbahce presidential rivals both want Greenwood
item.description: Former England and Manchester United striker Mason Greenwood is a key transfer target for both Fenerbahce's presidential candidates

item.title: Introducing Shorts on the BBC Sport app
item.description: From stunning goals and shocking interviews to unforgettable celebrations, some sporting moments are made to be watched, not just read about - and that's why BBC Sport are launching Shorts.

item.title: Barca & Real Madrid eye Cucurella - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Chelsea's Marc Cucurella linked with a return to Spain, Liverpool keen on teenage RB Leipzig forward Yan Diomande, and Newcastle face competition for Atalanta right-back Marco Palestra, plus more.

item.title: Why Liverpool want Iraola and what he could bring to Anfield
item.description: Why Liverpool believe Andoni Iraola is the man to re-establish the Reds as one of Europe's top clubs.

item.title: Overcoming resistance - Ancelotti's bid to revive Brazil
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti had to win over the Brazilian public and restore calm to chaos as he bids to return the country to World Cup glory.

item.title: Bellamy rejects club offers to commit to Wales
item.description: Craig Bellamy reiterates his commitment to Wales after revealing he has rejected job offers to manage club sides.

item.title: Earps & Leon poised to join London City Lionesses
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Mary Earps and Spain defender Mapi Leon are on the verge of joining London City Lionesses this summer.

item.title: Record-holder Milner retires after 24-year career
item.description: Former England international James Milner announces his retirement after a 24-year Premier League career.

item.title: Rodri to sort out Man City future after World Cup
item.description: Manchester City midfielder Rodri says he will wait until after the World Cup to sort out his future at the club amid links to Real Madrid.

item.title: Six people stabbed after Arsenal victory parade
item.description: The Metropolitan Police said 24 people were arrested on Sunday along or near the parade route.

item.title: I leave Liverpool exactly where it belongs - Slot
item.description: Arne Slot says he is leaving Liverpool "exactly where it belongs: among Europe's elite" after he was sacked as head coach on Saturday.

item.title: Chelsea sign McCabe after Arsenal exit
item.description: Chelsea confirm the signing of Republic of Ireland captain Katie McCabe following her exit from Arsenal.

item.title: Stewart hopeful for MND cure despite losing arm use
item.description: He believes time is on his side for a cure to be found.

item.title: Watch as Celtic beat Rangers in Women's Scottish Cup final
item.description: Watch as a Morgan Cross goal is enough to give Celtic victory over Rangers in the Women's Scottish Cup final despite a red card for Emma Lawton.

item.title: Watch Scotland put four past Curacao in World Cup send-off
item.description: Watch Scotland come from behind to beat 10-man Curacao in their World Cup send-off at Hampden.

item.title: How Slot went from champion to sack in a year
item.description: Just one year after lifting Liverpool's 20th league title in his debut season, Arne Slot has been sacked. BBC Sport looks back on how a dream start unravelled on Merseyside.

item.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
item.description: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.

item.title: Spurs secure Premier League survival with win over Everton
item.description: Joao Palhinha's goal just before half time helps Tottenham overcome Everton to secure the result they needed to ensure Premier League survival on a day of nerve-shredding tension.

item.title: The greatest World Cup shirts - and what makes them iconic
item.description: What are the best ever World Cup shirts and what makes them iconic? BBC Sport ranks the all-time top 10 here.

item.title: Williamson to miss World Cup qualifier against Spain
item.description: England captain Leah Williamson is ruled out of the Women's World Cup qualifying matches against Spain and Ukraine.

item.title: Price 'buzzing' after O'Neill opts to stay with NI
item.description: Northern Ireland midfielder Isaac Price expresses delight at Michael O'Neill remaining as manager of the international team and credits him with being a major influence on his career to date.

item.title: Who should be your World Cup second team?
item.description: The World Cup is almost here and you will no doubt have mapped out your team's potential route to the final, but if they exit early who should be your second team to follow at the tournament?

item.title: SWPL winners Hearts appoint Thomson as head coach
item.description: Andy Thomson is appointed head coach of Scottish Women Premier League champions Hearts, replacing Eva Olid.

item.title: Stockport boss Challinor leaves League One club
item.description: Stockport County manager Dave Challinor leaves the club by mutual consent after almost five years in charge.

item.title: Palace set to hold talks with Lens boss Sage
item.description: Lens manager Pierre Sage is set to hold talks with Crystal Palace as the Premier League club look to appoint Oliver Glasner's replacement.

item.title: West Ham relegated despite win over Leeds
item.description: West Ham's 14-year stay in the Premier League comes to an end after they were relegated to the Championship on the final day of the season despite beating Leeds at London Stadium.

item.title: Arsenal beat Palace before lifting Premier League trophy
item.description: Arsenal start their reign as Premier League champions with a routine win over Crystal Palace in a party atmosphere at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for Europa League
item.description: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for European competition for the first time in 53 years, securing a place in the Europa League at the visitors' expense.

item.title: Fernandes breaks Premier League record as Man Utd beat Brighton
item.description: Manchester United beat Brighton on the final day of the season, with captain Bruno Fernandes breaking the Premier League record for most assists in a single campaign.

item.title: Watkins & Villa spoil Guardiola's final Man City match
item.description: Pep Guardiola's 593rd and final game in charge of Manchester City ended in an emotional Premier League defeat as Ollie Watkins scored a double for Aston Villa.

item.title: Liverpool confirm Champions League spot with draw against Brentford
item.description: Liverpool confirm Champions League football for next season despite being held by Brentford as Anfield waved farewell to club legends Mohamed Salah and Andy Robertson.

item.title: Gibbs-White sends England message as Forest draw with Bournemouth
item.description: Nottingham Forest's Morgan Gibbs-White sends a message to England boss Thomas Tuchel, as Bournemouth miss out on the Champions League.

item.title: Fulham finish season with comfortable win over Newcastle
item.description: Substitute Thomas Cairney scores a superb long-range strike as Fulham signed off with a comfortable win against Newcastle United on the final day of the Premier League season.

item.title: Burnley and Wolves end Premier League campaigns with draw
item.description: Burnley and Wolves play out a 1-1 draw at Turf Moor to close out a miserable Premier League campaign for the two sides, who were relegated well in advance of the final day.

item.title: From £70 a week to Premier League record 24 years later - Milner retires
item.description: James Milner has broken the Premier League appearance record, 24 years after making his debut as a 16-year-old.

item.title: Hearts hope to seal Miller move from Falkirk
item.description: Heart of Midlothian are hopeful of concluding the signing of Falkirk winger Calvin Miller in the next 48 hours.

item.title: Ghana boss Queiroz has no qualms over Partey selection
item.description: Ghana coach Carlos Queiroz says he has no concerns selecting ex-Arsenal midfielder Thomas Partey for the World Cup.

item.title: Quiz: Name every nation to have played at a World Cup
item.description: Test whether you are able to name every team to have ever participated in a World Cup.

item.title: India's 'unsafe' 70ft Messi statue taken down
item.description: A 70ft statue of Lionel Messi in India is taken down after it was spotted "swaying in the wind" in May.

item.title: Joint-bosses: can Lincoln succeed where Liverpool failed?
item.description: Lincoln City's decision to appoint Chris Cohen and Tom Shaw as joint-head coaches is not an attempt to "break football convention", says Imps CEO Liam Scully.

item.title: The football fans who went to a World Cup and loved it so much, they stayed
item.description: A new documentary follows a group of Wolves fans who moved to the US after the Mexico World Cup.

item.title: Wolves rejection to double Champions League winner: Vitinha's rise to top
item.description: BBC Sport NI charts Vitinha's rise from an unsuccessful loan spell at Wolves to helping PSG win the Champions League twice in a row.

item.title: Unsung hero? Biggest disappointment? Fans rate your club's season
item.description: BBC Sport's Premier League club fan writers assess their team's performance as the domestic season comes to an end.

item.title: World Cup: Every squad as they are announced
item.description: Every squad for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico as they are announced.

item.title: Stick or twist for Arteta - how do Arsenal take next step?
item.description: Arsenal are fresh from their Champions League final heartbreak but thoughts are already moving to what they need to do to improve next season.

item.title: Premier League predictions - how accurate were BBC Sport pundits?
item.description: Last summer, 33 BBC pundits - plus AI and Opta's 'supercomputer' were asked to pick which teams would finish in the Premier League's top four, and in which order. How did they get on?

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: What would Crystal Palace winning Conference League mean for English clubs in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how Crystal Palace winning the Conference League would effect English teams entering Europe next season

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: Rangers' Berry and McAulay earn late Scotland call
item.description: Rangers' Laura Berry and Mia McAulay are added to the Scotland squad for Women's World Cup qualifiers against Israel after Como's Sophie Howard and Newcastle United's Freya Gregory drop out.

item.title: Is SWPL momentum with Celtic after Scottish Cup win?
item.description: What does Celtic's Women's Scottish Cup final win over Rangers mean for next season's SWPL title race?

item.title: 'Unacceptable' gap costing Wales young talent claim
item.description: Campaigners want to see Wales under-21 and 23 women's squads to improve pathways from age-grade to senior international football.

item.title: Man City's future looks bright - and Shaw's role is key
item.description: Manchester City's domestic double is the start of what they hope will be a hugely successful period for the club - and Khadija Shaw is at the centre of it.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.
